title: ACE Platform Architecture Tour
description: A guided tour of the ACE (Autonomous Capability Enhancement) platform — a hosted 'Playbooks as a Service' SaaS built on a three-agent self-improvement architecture with FastAPI, React, Celery, and MCP integration.

steps.title: Welcome to ACE Platform
steps.description: ACE Platform is a hosted service for self-improving AI playbooks. The codebase has four major areas highlighted here: the upstream three-agent algorithm, the backend with API routes, business logic, database models, an MCP server, and background workers, the frontend dashboard, and a comprehensive test suite. Configuration for containerization, deployment, and CI/CD rounds out the project.

steps.title: ACE Core: Three-Agent Architecture
steps.description: ACE runs a self-improvement loop with three agents. The **Generator** creates improved playbook versions, the **Reflector** evaluates changes against outcomes, and the **Curator** accepts, rejects, or modifies proposals. The highlighted orchestrator coordinates the full cycle.

interactiveActions.description: Open the main orchestrator to see how the three agents coordinate

steps.title: Platform API: FastAPI Routes
steps.description: The backend is a FastAPI application with 11 route modules. The highlighted entry point sets up middleware and lifecycle hooks. Routes are organized by domain — authentication, playbook CRUD and semantic matching, Stripe billing, and Google/GitHub OAuth.

interactiveActions.description: Explore the FastAPI app setup and middleware

steps.title: Business Logic & Services
steps.description: Twenty-nine service modules implement business logic decoupled from HTTP routing. The two highlight groups show the split between evolution-related services and the commercial billing and metering stack.

steps.title: Database & MCP Server
steps.description: The database layer defines ORM models with 14 migrations tracking schema changes. The MCP server exposes playbook operations as tools for AI assistants like Claude. Background workers handle asynchronous jobs such as playbook evolution and admin alerting.

interactiveActions.description: See the ORM models that define the database schema

steps.title: Frontend Dashboard
steps.description: A full-featured SaaS dashboard built with React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Shadcn UI. The highlighted pages area contains 21 route-based views, while shared components provide layout, playbook rendering, and subscription gating.

interactiveActions.description: Open the root component to see the route structure

steps.title: Testing, CI/CD & Deployment
steps.description: Over 54 test files cover the platform end-to-end. GitHub Actions auto-deploys to staging on every push to main, with manual production deploys. Highlighted scripts and configs manage the multi-stage container build and both environments.

interactiveActions.description: Browse the test suite to see coverage across the platform

steps.title: Putting It All Together
steps.description: You've now seen the full ACE Platform — from the three-agent self-improvement loop at its core, through the API and service layers that host it as a product, to the frontend that puts it in users' hands. The platform turns playbook evolution into a managed service with authentication, billing, usage metering, and background processing all wired together. Explore any area that caught your eye, or check the documentation for deeper dives.
